header.homeTooltip: Back to the main landing page
header.siteNavAriaLabel: Homepage, gallery and public site sections

hero.description: TrackDraw is a browser-based drone race track builder for FPV race directors. Draw to scale in 2D, review the flow in 3D, share a read-only layout with pilots and crew, and keep the plan usable from desktop or mobile.
hero.videoAlt: TrackDraw drone race track builder demo
hero.videoDescription: A quick product demo showing how TrackDraw helps FPV race directors build a drone race track layout, review it in 3D, and share it with pilots and crew.

features.sectionDescription: TrackDraw gives FPV race directors a practical way to design tracks properly: real field dimensions, the right obstacle set, and one workflow that carries the same plan from layout to review to team hand-off.

trueToScale.title: True-to-scale canvas
trueToScale.description: Set the field size, place elements to scale, and work on a grid that matches the real venue.

obstacleCatalog.title: Official obstacle catalog
obstacleCatalog.description: Place official MultiGP obstacles (gates, flags, ladders, hurdles, and barriers) with real dimensions and accurate 3D rendering.

preview3d.title: Realistic 3D preview
preview3d.description: Orbit in 3D, add elevation to the race line, review maneuver curves, and fly through the route to catch flow problems before build day.

shareLinks.title: Shared review links
shareLinks.description: Share a read-only layout with pilots and crew. Guest links are temporary; account-published links can stay live until revoked.

sections.title: Track sections
sections.description: Save any canvas selection as a named section and place it again with one click. Sections sync with your account across devices.

export.title: Race Pack and export
export.description: Generate a pilot briefing PDF with obstacle layout, route map, and setup notes. Also export SVG, PNG, and JSON from the same design.

workflow.sectionHeading: From first layout to team hand-off.
workflow.sectionDescription: The tool works best as a sequence: map the drone race track in 2D, check it in 3D, make practical edits when reality changes, then hand the same plan to pilots and crew.

layout.title: 2D Layout
layout.subtitle: Build the track on a field that matches reality.
layout.description: Start with the field dimensions, then place the elements crews already recognize. That makes the first version of the plan useful immediately, instead of just being a rough sketch.
layout.bullet2: Gates, flags, cones, start pads and larger features
layout.bullet3: Race line and labels for briefing clarity

fineTune.title: Fine-Tune
fineTune.subtitle: Keep editing when the venue forces a change.
fineTune.description: A good plan changes cleanly. Inspector controls and mobile-friendly editing make it possible to adjust object properties, reposition items and keep the design usable even when the real venue refuses to stay static.
fineTune.bullet1: Inspector controls for dimensions, rotation and color
fineTune.bullet2: Touch-friendly editing on phones and tablets
fineTune.bullet3: Fast adjustments without leaving the main plan

preview.title: 3D Preview
preview.subtitle: Walk the track before you build it.
preview.description: One click switches from the flat 2D plan to a live 3D scene. Assign elevation to each waypoint on the race line to model the full vertical profile. Catch dangerous approaches before a single peg is in the ground.
preview.bullet1: Live 3D rendered directly from your 2D design
preview.bullet2: Per-waypoint altitude on the race line
preview.bullet3: Elevation graph in the inspector panel

handOff.title: Hand-Off
handOff.subtitle: Give pilots and crew the same current version.
handOff.description: A plan only matters if other people can open it. Shared read-only links and exports turn the layout into something pilots can review, crew can print and organizers can reuse later.
handOff.bullet1: Read-only links that open on any device
handOff.bullet2: PDF, PNG and SVG for briefing and print workflows
handOff.bullet3: JSON export to archive and reuse layouts later

faq.q1: Is TrackDraw a drone race track builder?
faq.a1: Yes. TrackDraw is a browser-based drone race track builder for FPV race directors who need scale-accurate layouts, 3D review, sharing, and export in one workflow.
faq.q2: What is the best way to plan an FPV race track?
faq.a2: Start with the field dimensions, place the obstacles you actually have, then review the route in 3D before sharing or exporting the plan. TrackDraw is built around that exact sequence.
faq.q3: Can pilots review the layout without an account?
faq.a3: Yes. Share links open in read-only mode on any device, no account or app needed. Send it to pilots, judges, or crew and everyone sees the same published version.
faq.q4: Does it work on a tablet at the venue?
faq.a4: Yes. The editor is touch-friendly, so quick adjustments on a phone or tablet are practical when the real venue forces a change.
faq.a5: PDF, PNG, SVG, JSON, and a cinematic FPV video export. That covers printed race briefs, shareable visuals, reusable project files, and motion-led route review.
faq.q6: Do I need an account to get started?
faq.a6: No. Open the studio and start designing right away. Creating an account adds cloud storage, durable published links, gallery publishing, and embeds.
faq.q7: Can I reuse a layout for a future event?
faq.a7: Yes. Save parts of a layout as named presets and place them again in one click. To reuse a full project, export it as JSON and import it at the start of a new event.
faq.q8: Can TrackDraw help catch route problems before race day?
faq.a8: Yes. The 3D preview and route review warnings help surface uneven spacing, rhythm breaks, and alignment issues before you share the layout or start building it.

pricing.sectionHeading: Start now, sign up when you need more.
pricing.sectionDescription: Both current options are free. Guest mode keeps the core editor open without setup; accounts add durable publishing, embeds, gallery listing, and project continuity across devices.

guest.name: Guest
guest.description: Best for quick drafts, one-off temporary review links, and local export without creating an account.

account.name: Account
account.description: Best for durable published links, embeds, gallery, track sections, REST API access, and cross-device project continuity.

features.shareLinksNote: Guest share links are temporary. Account-published links stay live until revoked and can also be embedded.
features.embedsDetail: Embed on any website via iframe
features.cloudProjectsDetail: Projects sync with your account so you can continue on any device — from desktop at home to mobile at the venue.
features.sectionsDetail: Save any canvas selection as a named preset and place it again from the preset picker. Syncs with your account across devices.

footer.tagline: Browser-based track designer for FPV race directors. Draw to scale, preview in 3D, share a read-only link, and keep quick edits moving on mobile.
footer.copyright: © {year} Dutch Drone Squad. Open source project.

gallery.metaTitle: TrackDraw | FPV Drone Race Track Gallery
gallery.metaDescription: Browse FPV drone race track designs shared by the TrackDraw community.
gallery.description: Browse public TrackDraw layouts shared by the community for inspiration. Featured tracks surface the strongest examples first, with recent community uploads right below.

notFound.heading: This page drifted off the course
notFound.description: The page you requested does not exist, may have moved, or may have taken a wrong turn at the gate. The latest track or route should be close by.
notFound.step1: Jump back to the studio and open the latest track.
notFound.step2: Return home if you came here from an old bookmark.
notFound.step3: Try the latest route again if the page moved recently.
